title: Implement SQLite database layer with sqlx
description: In crates/teamserver: database module using sqlx with SQLite. Write migrations for all tables: ts_agents (full schema from Havoc), ts_listeners, ts_links (pivot relationships), ts_loot, ts_audit_log (new). Implement repository structs with async CRUD methods for each table. Use sqlx::migrate! macro. Connection pool setup. Write integration tests.
close_reason: Implemented SQLite sqlx database layer, migrations, repositories, and integration tests

title: Embed Python runtime in teamserver via PyO3
description: In crates/teamserver: embed a Python 3.x interpreter using PyO3. Initialize the Python VM at startup. Load .py module files from a configured plugins directory. Expose a Python API module (import red_cell) with bindings for: agent access (get_agent, list_agents), listener access, event subscription (on_agent_checkin, on_command_output), and command registration. Thread-safe GIL management with tokio.
close_reason: Embedded a PyO3 runtime in teamserver startup, added plugin loading from Teamserver.PluginsDir, and exposed the initial red_cell Python API with agent/listener access plus event and command registration hooks.

title: parse_hour_minute validation off-by-one accepts invalid times 24:xx and xx:60
description: In crates/teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs, the parse_hour_minute function at line 1066 checks 'if hour > 24 || minute > 60'. This is off-by-one: hour 24 is not a valid 24-hour clock hour (valid range is 0–23), and minute 60 is not a valid minute (valid range is 0–59). An operator submitting WorkingHours '24:60-00:01' passes validation and gets packed into the agent config. Correct fix: change the guard to 'if hour > 23 || minute > 59'.
close_reason: Fixed off-by-one: changed guard from hour>24||minute>60 to hour>23||minute>59. Added 5 boundary tests.

title: Teamserver - Demon Agent Protocol
description: Full implementation of the Demon binary C2 protocol for agent communication. Must maintain backward compatibility with existing compiled Demon agents: 0xDEADBEEF magic, AES-256-CBC per-agent encryption, binary packet format, all command IDs (DEMON_INIT=99, COMMAND_CHECKIN=100, COMMAND_GET_JOB=1, etc). Agent cannot be modified.

title: Zero-key bypass in DEMON_INIT allows unauthenticated plaintext agent registration
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:253 — parse_init_agent() treats any DEMON_INIT packet where the 32-byte AES key field is all-zeros as a plaintext payload (no decryption). Any process that can reach the agent-facing HTTP/SMB listener port can craft a valid DEMON_INIT with a zeroed key, register as an agent without possessing the pre-shared implant key, and receive a plaintext init-ack.

crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:179 — build_init_ack() mirrors this: if the stored key is all-zeros, the ack (containing the agent_id) is returned as plaintext, confirming the handshake for a zero-key 'agent'.

The bypass is inherited from the Havoc debug/test code path and is intentional for development convenience, but it exists unchanged in production code. A threat actor who can reach the listener endpoint (e.g. because the listener is internet-exposed for real agents) can use it to register phantom agents without a properly-keyed implant binary.

Fix: reject DEMON_INIT packets with an all-zero key in production builds, or gate the bypass behind an explicit profile-level debug flag (e.g. Demon { AllowPlaintextInit = false }). At minimum, log a prominent warning when a zero-key init is accepted.
close_reason: Rejected all-zero AES keys during DEMON_INIT and agent transport ack handling.

title: parse_decrypts_callback_packages_for_existing_agent will decrypt garbage after CTR fix
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-2bm)

crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs — test parse_decrypts_callback_packages_for_existing_agent

The test builds the callback packet using:
  encrypt_agent_data(&key, &iv, &decrypted)  // always starts at CTR offset=0

But after processing the DEMON_INIT, the server CTR offset is set to:
  ctr_blocks_for_length(encrypted_metadata_len) = 12 blocks (182-byte metadata)

decrypt_from_agent will attempt to decrypt the callback at offset=12, but the data was encrypted at offset=0. Decryption produces garbage, which will cause parse_callback_packages to fail with BufferTooShort or wrong data.

This test is currently unreachable due to red-3tc (compile failure). Once that is fixed, this test will fail.

Fix: Update build_callback_packet to accept a block_offset parameter and use encrypt_agent_data_ctr(key, iv, offset, data) so the callback is encrypted at the correct post-init offset.
close_reason: Fixed as part of AES-CTR IV reuse fix (red-2bm)

title: Agent ID 0 in DemonInit header bypasses identity mismatch check
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:260 — The condition `if parsed_agent_id != agent_id && agent_id != 0` allows a packet with `agent_id = 0` in the transport header to skip the check that the header ID matches the decrypted payload ID. An attacker can craft a `DEMON_INIT` packet with header `agent_id = 0` and an arbitrary `parsed_agent_id` in the encrypted payload, bypassing the mismatch guard and registering a spoofed agent.

Fix: remove the `agent_id != 0` exception entirely. The guard should always require the header and payload IDs to match when both are present.
close_reason: Removed agent_id != 0 exception from DemonInit identity mismatch guard; added regression test

title: parse_dns_query does not reject DNS response packets (QR bit unchecked)
close_reason: Reject DNS packets with QR=1 in parse_dns_query and cover with regression test

title: DnsListenerState::responses map leaks memory — completed downloads never removed
description:
In listeners.rs, DnsListenerState::handle_download returns the string 'done' when seq >= total (i.e. the agent has polled past the last chunk), but it never removes the corresponding entry from self.responses. The responses HashMap therefore accumulates one entry per agent per checkin cycle and is never cleaned up while the listener is running.

Unlike self.uploads (which is pruned by a retain call on every new upload chunk), self.responses has no expiry or cleanup path. In an active engagement with many agents checking in frequently, this is an unbounded memory leak.

Fix: remove the agent's entry from self.responses once 'done' is returned. Since handle_download takes a shared read lock, the removal should either:
  (a) upgrade to a write lock when idx >= total, remove the entry, and return 'done', or
  (b) change the data structure so that entries are removed from the write side (handle_upload) after a timeout, similar to how uploads are expired.

Location: crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs, DnsListenerState::handle_download, around line 1453.
close_reason: Fixed by removing completed DNS download responses after returning done; verified with regression test and full workspace validation.

title: Implement RBAC system (roles and permissions)
description: In crates/teamserver: role-based access control. Define roles: Admin (full access), Operator (can task agents, manage listeners), Analyst (read-only: view agents, sessions, loot; no tasking). Roles configurable per operator in the profile. Middleware/extractor that checks permissions on each WebSocket command and REST endpoint. Write unit tests covering permission boundaries.
close_reason: Implemented operator roles, RBAC extractors, websocket authorization, and boundary tests

title: demon.rs: build_init_ack test uses stale sync API — cargo test fails to compile
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-2bm)

crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:641-653 — test build_init_ack_encrypts_agent_identifier

build_init_ack was refactored from:
  pub fn build_init_ack(agent_id: u32, encryption: &AgentEncryptionInfo) -> Result<Vec<u8>, DemonParserError>
to:
  pub async fn build_init_ack(registry: &crate::AgentRegistry, agent_id: u32) -> Result<Vec<u8>, DemonParserError>

The test still calls the old signature build_init_ack(0xAABB_CCDD, &encryption) and uses #[test] instead of #[tokio::test]. This causes two compile errors:
  E0308: wrong argument count/types
  E0599: .expect() called on a Future, not a Result

Result: cargo test --workspace fails to compile the lib test binary. All 385 tests in the teamserver crate are unreachable.

Fix: Update the test to use a test registry, change to #[tokio::test], and call build_init_ack(&registry, agent_id).await.
close_reason: Fixed as part of AES-CTR IV reuse fix (red-2bm)

title: REST API handler tests do not cover update, delete, start, stop, or mark endpoints
description: The test module in crates/teamserver/src/api.rs covers: GET / (root), GET /listeners (auth required, role check, rate limit), GET /openapi.json, and 404 fallback. Missing coverage: PUT /listeners/{name} (update), DELETE /listeners/{name}, POST /listeners/{name}/start, POST /listeners/{name}/stop, and POST /listeners/{name}/mark. Each of these has distinct success/error paths (e.g. 404 not-found, 409 already-running, 422 start failure). Add unit tests for each handler exercising at least the happy path and the most important error case.
close_reason: Added 14 unit tests covering all five missing REST API listener endpoints (update, delete, start, stop, mark) with happy paths and error cases

title: dispatch: parallel builtin dispatch chains risk divergence when adding pivot callbacks
description: dispatch.rs lines 706-875: dispatch_builtin_packages + dispatch_builtin_package implement a full parallel copy of the builtin handler chain (as a plain if-chain) alongside CommandDispatcher::with_builtin_handlers_and_max_download_bytes. Any new handler added to one path must be manually mirrored in the other. Introduced in commit a4d0ad9 (feat: implement SMB pivot management) by michel@woodenshoe.org. Currently they are in sync but the architecture makes future divergence likely. P3 — no current functional impact, maintenance risk.
close_reason: Shared builtin handler registration between the main dispatcher and pivot callback dispatch path to remove the duplicate chain.

title: DoS: HTTP listener buffers entire agent request body with no size limit
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1831 — The HTTP listener handler calls `to_bytes(body, usize::MAX)` before any authentication or size validation. An unauthenticated attacker can send a multi-GB HTTP request body to the agent-facing listener and exhaust server memory.

The magic-value and minimum-length check (`is_valid_demon_callback_request`) only runs *after* the entire body is already buffered in memory.

Fix: apply an upper-bound limit matching the maximum expected Demon payload size (e.g., the existing `DEMON_MAX_RESPONSE_LENGTH = 0x01E0_0000` constant) before buffering the body.
close_reason: Bounded HTTP demon callback body reads to the existing ~30 MiB protocol limit and added an oversized-request regression test.

title: plugins: invoke_registered_command broadcasts AgentTask with unrelated task_id
description: In plugins.rs::invoke_registered_command (line 517), the operator broadcast AgentTask message is given a new task_id via next_request_id(). However, the actual queued job (enqueued during the Python callback via agent.task()) was already assigned task_id and request_id from earlier next_request_id() calls inside queue_raw_agent_task. The broadcast task_id is a completely different counter value and will never match the queued job's task_id, breaking operator-side task tracking and output correlation. The fix is to capture the task_id at enqueue time and thread it through to the broadcast.
close_reason: Fixed: invoke_registered_command now captures the task_id from queue_raw_agent_task via a shared Arc<Mutex> on PyAgent and reuses it in the AgentTask broadcast, ensuring operator-side task tracking matches the queued job.

title: Unused import DemonCallback in dispatch.rs produces compiler warning
description:
dispatch.rs imports DemonCallback from red_cell_common::demon but does not use it anywhere. This generates a compiler warning. Either use it in the callback handler implementations (likely the correct fix once handle_filesystem_callback and handle_beacon_output_callback are implemented to dispatch on DemonCallback variants), or remove the import.

Location: crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:13
close_reason: No longer reproduces: DemonCallback is used throughout dispatch.rs (try_from on line 1223, match arms lines 1229-1363, and in tests). cargo check and cargo clippy both pass with zero warnings.

title: SOCKS5 relay binds to 0.0.0.0, exposing proxy to the whole network
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:103 — add_socks_server unconditionally binds to 0.0.0.0:

    let bind_addr = format!("0.0.0.0:{port}");

This means any host on the network can connect to the teamserver's SOCKS5 relay and pivot through the agent, without any authentication (the SOCKS5 implementation selects NO_AUTH only). The bind address should default to 127.0.0.1 so only local operator tools can use it.

Fix: change the bind_addr to 127.0.0.1:{port}. A configurable bind address could be exposed via the SocketRelayManager API if remote access is needed.
close_reason: Changed the SOCKS5 relay default bind address to 127.0.0.1 and added a regression test for the loopback listener.

title: plugins: emit_agent_checkin and emit_command_output lack unit tests
description: The two public async methods PluginRuntime::emit_agent_checkin() and PluginRuntime::emit_command_output() in crates/teamserver/src/plugins.rs (lines 146 and 155) have no unit tests. These are the core event-emission entry points called by dispatch.rs; they need tests that: (1) verify callbacks registered via on_agent_checkin/on_command_output are actually invoked when emitted, and (2) verify errors are propagated correctly when a callback raises a Python exception.
close_reason: Added 4 unit tests for emit_agent_checkin and emit_command_output: callback invocation, command output payload, unknown agent early return, and exception swallowing

title: Add per-IP DEMON_INIT rate limiting for agent-facing listeners
description: Registration-cap enforcement prevents unbounded agent growth, but internet-exposed HTTP/SMB/DNS listeners still accept unlimited DEMON_INIT attempts from a single source IP until the cap is reached. Add a dedicated per-IP limiter for DEMON_INIT acceptance on agent-facing listeners, separate from operator login throttling, and cover HTTP/SMB/DNS listener paths with tests.
close_reason: Added a dedicated per-IP DEMON_INIT limiter for HTTP, SMB, and DNS listeners with end-to-end coverage.

title: No test coverage for DownloadTracker size limits or multi-chunk accumulation
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs — DownloadTracker impl has no unit tests.

The DownloadTracker (append / start / finish) and the FileOpen→FileWrite→
FileClose state machine in the BeaconOutput and CommandFs::Download
handlers have no dedicated tests.  In particular:

* no test exercises what happens when an agent reports expected_size much
  larger than the actual data sent (the download never completes);
* no test exercises what happens when FileWrite chunks arrive with no
  preceding FileOpen (should error, but DownloadTracker.append returns
  InvalidCallbackPayload);
* no test exercises that exceeding any future size cap is enforced.

These paths are implicated in red-aow (OOM from rogue agent) and would
have caught that bug if a test existed.

Fix: add unit tests for DownloadTracker directly, and add integration
tests for the FileOpen→FileWrite→FileClose sequence including abnormal
orderings.
close_reason: Added DownloadTracker unit tests plus Beacon/CommandFs multi-chunk and invalid-order download coverage

title: No unit tests for encrypt_for_agent / decrypt_from_agent / ctr_offset — new CTR functions untested
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-2bm)

crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs — functions added in red-2bm:
  - encrypt_for_agent (line ~316)
  - decrypt_from_agent (line ~340)
  - ctr_offset (line ~295)
  - set_ctr_offset (line ~302)

None of these new functions have direct unit tests. The existing encryption_round_trips test (line 1028) only tests get/set_encryption, not the CTR-tracked encrypt/decrypt path.

Missing coverage:
  - Successive encrypt_for_agent calls advance CTR monotonically
  - encrypt_for_agent + decrypt_from_agent round-trip (counter must match)
  - set_ctr_offset + subsequent encrypt uses the new offset
  - Zero-key pass-through (Secure=false agent) returns plaintext unchanged
  - Unknown agent_id returns AgentNotFound error

Fix: Add tokio::test cases for these functions covering all the above scenarios.
close_reason: Added direct async coverage for CTR-tracked agent encryption helpers and zero-key pass-through.

title: dispatch.rs: token steal callback message says "Successful stole" instead of "Successfully stole"
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-HTB-cursor (Cursor Agent)

In `crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:2278`, the `DemonTokenCommand::Steal` callback formats:

  "Successful stole and impersonated token from {target_pid} User:[{user}] TokenID:[{token_id}]"

This uses the adjective "Successful" where the adverb "Successfully" is needed. The same class of grammar error was fixed by Cursor in a prior task (red-cell-c2-f0d), but this new message was introduced in the same batch and not caught.

The test `token_steal_callback_broadcasts_success_event` only checks that the message contains "stole and impersonated token from 1234", so it does not catch the grammar error.
close_reason: Corrected the token steal success message grammar and tightened the regression test to assert the full callback text.

title: SOCKS5 domain-name response silently truncates addresses longer than 255 bytes
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:617

send_socks_connect_reply() emits the SOCKS5 ATYP_DOMAIN response like:
  response.push(u8::try_from(address.len()).unwrap_or_default());

If the domain name is longer than 255 bytes, try_from fails and
unwrap_or_default() writes 0x00 as the length byte.  The full domain bytes
are still appended after it, so the SOCKS5 response is structurally
corrupt: the client reads 0 bytes as the domain field and treats the
remaining bytes as port + data — producing a garbage reply.

RFC 1928 § 5 says the domain-name length field is a single ATYP_DOMAIN
octet, so >255-byte names are technically illegal.  The correct behavior
is to refuse the CONNECT with a SOCKS_REPLY_ADDRESS_TYPE_NOT_SUPPORTED or
SOCKS_REPLY_GENERAL_FAILURE reply instead of silently corrupting the
response frame.

Fix: before writing the domain bytes, check address.len() <= 255; if not,
send a failure reply and return early.
close_reason: Rejected oversized SOCKS5 domain replies with a failure frame and added a regression test.

title: plugins: large WIP in git stash is not committed — work may be lost
description: git stash list shows stash@{0} on main at 75da201 containing ~536 net changes to crates/teamserver/src/plugins.rs, crates/teamserver/src/main.rs, and crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs. This is significant in-progress work for red-cell-c2-25h (Python API bindings) that was never committed. The stash was likely left accidentally after a QA pull. The dev agent should: (1) apply the stash, (2) review the diff, (3) commit or integrate the changes. If left as-is the work risks being dropped.
close_reason: Recovered orphaned plugin stash by archiving it to branch recovered/red-cell-c2-25h-stash and confirming its work landed in f903f5c

title: request_id silently defaults to 0 when task_id is not valid hex
description: In crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs line 599: u32::from_str_radix(info.task_id.trim(), 16).unwrap_or_default() — if the operator sends a task_id that is not a valid hex string (empty, malformed, or decimal), request_id silently becomes 0. This means correlation between operator task and agent response is lost without any warning. Fix: return an error or log a warning when task_id cannot be parsed, rather than silently defaulting to 0.
close_reason: Replaced unwrap_or_default() with proper error propagation via InvalidTaskId variant. Empty, non-hex, and overflowing task_id values now return AgentCommandError::InvalidTaskId instead of silently defaulting request_id to 0.

title: AgentSocketState map never pruned when agents die — unbounded SOCKS5 relay memory growth
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:81 — SocketRelayManager.state is a HashMap<u32, AgentSocketState> keyed by agent_id. When an agent is killed, removed, or goes inactive, its entry in this map is never removed.

Over a long operation, every agent that has ever had SOCKS or reverse-proxy activity leaves a dead entry in this map. The AgentSocketState struct contains BTreeMap<u16, SocksServerHandle> (server handles) and HashMap<u32, PendingClient> (open sockets), so leaked entries can hold OS resources as well.

Fix: hook into the agent removal path to call a cleanup method on SocketRelayManager, or add a periodic sweep that removes entries for agent_ids that are no longer in the AgentRegistry.
close_reason: Added socket relay cleanup on agent removal plus stale-agent pruning for inactive agents.

title: handle_process_command_callback errors on Modules, Grep, Memory subcommands
description: crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs handle_process_command_callback (registered for COMMAND_PROC) only handles DemonProcessCommand::Create (=4) and DemonProcessCommand::Kill (=7). The other defined subcommands — Modules=2, Grep=3, Memory=6 — fall into the 'other' arm and return CommandDispatchError::InvalidCallbackPayload. If the Demon agent sends any of these callbacks, the response is dropped and the operator sees nothing. At minimum log and emit a generic operator event, or implement the full handlers.
close_reason: Implemented full Modules, Grep, and Memory process subcommand callback handlers with formatted operator output, structured JSON extras, and 4 new tests

title: crypto.rs: ctr_blocks_for_length uses manual div_ceil — clippy lint failure
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-2bm)

crates/common/src/crypto.rs, function ctr_blocks_for_length (uncommitted local change):

    let blocks = (data_len + AES_BLOCK_LEN - 1) / AES_BLOCK_LEN;

clippy::manual_div_ceil fires under `-D warnings`, causing the workspace to fail to compile with clippy.

Fix: replace with `data_len.div_ceil(AES_BLOCK_LEN)` per the clippy suggestion. This is a Rust 1.73+ stable API.
close_reason: Fixed as part of AES-CTR IV reuse fix (red-2bm)

title: No cap on agent registry size — unauthenticated DEMON_INIT flood exhausts memory and SQLite
description:
Introduced by: Codex (original agent registry design)

crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs — AgentRegistry.entries (HashMap<u32, Arc<AgentEntry>>) has no maximum capacity. Every DEMON_INIT received by an HTTP, SMB, or DNS listener inserts a new entry and a corresponding SQLite row (via database::AgentRepository::create).

An attacker who can reach the agent-facing listener port (which is intentionally internet-exposed for real implants) can flood DEMON_INIT packets with distinct agent_id values (u32 → 4 billion distinct IDs). Each fake registration:
- allocates an AgentEntry with its own RwLock, Mutex, VecDeque, and HashMap
- persists an agents row + encryption row to SQLite
- broadcasts an AgentNew event to all connected operators

Combined with the zero-key bypass (red-2d4), crafting valid registration packets requires no crypto knowledge. Without a rate limit or max-agent cap, a single attacker machine can OOM the teamserver in minutes.

Fix: add a configurable MAX_REGISTERED_AGENTS limit (suggested default: 10 000) to AgentRegistry::insert, returning an error when exceeded. Also consider per-IP rate limiting on DEMON_INIT acceptance (distinct from the operator login rate limiter).
close_reason: Added a configurable MaxRegisteredAgents limit, enforced registry insert rejection at capacity, and covered the DEMON_INIT path with tests.

title: Implement Python API bindings for teamserver
description: In crates/teamserver: full Python API surface. RegisterCommand(name, description, callback): adds custom command visible to operators. RegisterCallback(event_type, callback): calls Python on agent events. Agent object: .task(command, args), .info (hostname, user, etc). Listener object: .start(), .stop(). Compatible with existing Havoc Python module conventions where possible. Write example module and tests.
close_reason: Implemented Havoc-compatible teamserver Python bindings, plugin command execution, listener controls, example module, and tests

title: LoginRateLimiter windows map grows unboundedly under IPs never seen again
description:
Introduced by: Cursor Agent <noreply@cursor.com>

crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs — LoginRateLimiter (commit 1aa5dd6)

The LoginRateLimiter::windows HashMap is pruned lazily: expired entries are only removed when the same IP makes a follow-up request (is_allowed/record_failure). An adversary probing from many distinct source IPs—or a coordinated DoS scan—inserts one LoginAttemptWindow per IP and those entries are never evicted without a second request from the same IP. Over time the operator-facing port accumulates O(attacker IPs) entries with no bound.

This is the same class of bug as red-pkl (AgentSocketState) and red-aow (DownloadTracker). Even though the operator port is not publicly advertised, the teamserver may be reachable from monitored network ranges and this provides a steady memory-exhaustion path.

Fix: add a max-size eviction pass (similar to evict_oldest_contexts in agents.rs) in record_failure, OR launch a background tokio::spawn task that sweeps and removes expired windows every LOGIN_WINDOW_DURATION. Cap total map size at, e.g., 10,000 entries.
close_reason: Pruned expired login windows and capped tracked IP entries in LoginRateLimiter.

title: add_bytes silently emits zero-length prefix on slice > 4 GiB instead of returning error
description: In crates/teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs at line 1088, the helper 'add_bytes' calls 'u32::try_from(value.len()).unwrap_or_default()'. If the slice ever exceeds u32::MAX (~4 GiB), the length prefix is silently written as 0, corrupting the packed config binary stream rather than propagating an error. Should propagate a PayloadBuildError::InvalidRequest instead. In practice payloads are never this large, but the silent truncation is an incorrect invariant: the code should panic or return Err, never silently proceed with corrupt data.
close_reason: add_bytes and add_wstring now return Result<(), PayloadBuildError>; slice length exceeding u32::MAX returns InvalidRequest instead of silently writing 0

title: build_dns_txt_response hardcodes TXT QTYPE in question section regardless of original query type
description:
In listeners.rs, build_dns_txt_response reconstructs the question section of the DNS response by writing qname_raw followed by DNS_TYPE_TXT (16) and DNS_CLASS_IN. However, the original query's QTYPE is not passed in, so if the query was an A record query (QTYPE=1), the response question section claims QTYPE=TXT (16), which violates RFC 1035 §4.1.2 (the question section in a response must echo the original question).

The ParsedDnsQuery struct already contains a qtype field. The fix is to add a qtype parameter to build_dns_txt_response (or pass the full ParsedDnsQuery) and use query.qtype instead of DNS_TYPE_TXT when writing the question section.

Priority 3 — this is a protocol correctness issue but doesn't affect C2 functionality since all C2 traffic uses TXT queries, and the current code always responds with a TXT answer regardless.
close_reason: Preserved the original DNS query QTYPE when rebuilding TXT response questions and added a regression test.

title: handle_token_callback returns error for all subcommands except Impersonate
description: crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs handle_token_callback (registered for COMMAND_TOKEN) only handles DemonTokenCommand::Impersonate and propagates a CommandDispatchError for every other subcommand (Steal=2, List=3, PrivsGetOrList=4, Make=5, GetUid=6, Revert=7, Remove=8, Clear=9, FindTokens=10). When the Demon agent sends back a callback for any of those subcommands, the handler fails, causing the response to be silently dropped (the error is logged but no operator event is emitted). Either implement the remaining subcommands or handle them with a generic/no-op response rather than returning an error that kills the callback.
close_reason: Implemented all token subcommand callbacks (Steal, List, PrivsGetOrList, Make, GetUid, Revert, Remove, Clear, FindTokens) with protocol-compatible parsing matching the Havoc Demon agent. Also updated encode_token_payload and token_subcommand to support all subcommands from the operator side. Added 12 tests covering all subcommands.

title: DNS listener response map (DnsPendingResponse) has no expiry, causing unbounded memory growth
description:
In listeners.rs, DnsListenerState contains a 'responses: Mutex<HashMap<u32, DnsPendingResponse>>' that is populated when an agent upload is processed successfully. Entries are never removed: handle_download returns 'done' when the agent has polled all chunks but does not call responses.remove(&agent_id). Under active C2 use, every completed agent interaction leaves a stale entry in this map.

By contrast, the uploads map does expire entries via 'uploads.retain(|_, p| p.received_at.elapsed().as_secs() < DNS_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS)' called in try_assemble_upload.

Fix: either (a) remove the entry from responses inside handle_download when idx >= total (converting &self to &mut self or using interior mutability properly), or (b) add a similar time-based expiry by adding a received_at timestamp to DnsPendingResponse and purging in handle_download or a background cleanup task.
close_reason: Removed completed DNS pending responses on terminal download polls and added a regression test.

title: No end-to-end integration test: HTTP listener → parser → dispatcher pipeline
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/tests/ contains only database.rs. There is no integration test that exercises the full path: HTTP listener receives a Demon packet → DemonPacketParser parses it → CommandDispatcher routes the callback → operator event is broadcast.

All components are unit-tested in isolation, but a wiring error (e.g., the listener passing the wrong field as external_ip, or the dispatcher failing to find the agent) would not be caught.

Fix: add an integration test that starts a real HTTP listener on a loopback port, sends a synthesized DEMON_INIT followed by a COMMAND_CHECKIN, and asserts that the agent appears in the registry and the correct operator event is broadcast.
close_reason: Added a real HTTP listener integration test covering DEMON_INIT registration, registry state, and COMMAND_CHECKIN event dispatch.

title: encrypt_agent_data silently returns empty Vec on invalid key or IV length
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/common/src/crypto.rs:51-52 — `encrypt_agent_data` swallows the `CryptoError` returned by `apply_agent_keystream` and returns an empty `Vec<u8>` instead. Callers in `agents.rs:522` and `dispatch.rs:448` build serialized job payloads using the return value without checking whether encryption actually succeeded.

If a stored key or IV is malformed (e.g., a mis-encoded base64 value), the agent will silently receive an empty encrypted response instead of an error being surfaced to the operator.

Fix: change the return type of `encrypt_agent_data` to `Result<Vec<u8>, CryptoError>` and update all call sites to propagate the error.
notes: Cursor agent (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor) claimed this task but ran out of credits before starting implementation. Working tree was left clean — no code changes were made. Pick up from scratch.
close_reason: Changed encrypt_agent_data to return Result and updated callers/tests to surface crypto errors

title: Typo "impersonat" in token callback error message
description:
In crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs line 479, the error message reads "Failed to impersonat {user}" — missing the trailing "e" in "impersonate". This is displayed verbatim in the operator console.

Fix: change to "Failed to impersonate {user}".
close_reason: Typo already fixed — line 2262 of dispatch.rs correctly reads 'Failed to impersonate {user}'

title: Build broken: 3 filesystem callback handlers called but not defined
description:
dispatch.rs calls handle_filesystem_callback, handle_command_output_callback, and handle_beacon_output_callback in two places each, but none of these functions are defined anywhere in the codebase. This causes 6 compile errors and prevents the workspace from building.

Affected locations:
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:172 (handle_filesystem_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:205 (handle_command_output_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:220 (handle_beacon_output_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:611 (handle_filesystem_callback in route_callback_by_command)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:621 (handle_command_output_callback in route_callback_by_command)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:624 (handle_beacon_output_callback in route_callback_by_command)

These must be implemented as async fns in dispatch.rs to fix the build. The filesystem callback should parse DemonCallback variants (CALLBACK_FILE for chunked download reassembly, CALLBACK_FILE_NOTFOUND, CALLBACK_OUTPUT, etc.) and broadcast results to operators. See the DemonCallback enum in common for the expected message types.
close_reason: Implemented the missing filesystem callback handlers and fixed shared download tracker reuse in builtin dispatch

title: E2E test fails after CTR fix: operator_session_listener_and_mock_demon_round_trip decrypts at wrong offset
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-2bm)

crates/teamserver/tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs:119-120

After Cursor's CTR-tracking refactor, build_init_ack now calls registry.encrypt_for_agent(agent_id, &payload) which encrypts the ack at CTR offset N (where N = ctr_blocks_for_length(init_encrypted_len), set at demon.rs:149 during DEMON_INIT processing).

The E2E test still decrypts the ack at offset 0:
  let init_ack = decrypt_agent_data(&key, &iv, &init_bytes)?;

This produces garbage (actual [76,123,184,63] vs expected [120,86,52,18] = 0x12345678 in LE) and the test fails.

The integration tests in listeners.rs mod tests were correctly updated to use CTR-tracked decryption (advance_iv + ctr_blocks_for_length), but the E2E test in tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs was missed.

cargo test --workspace currently fails: 'operator_session_listener_and_mock_demon_round_trip' FAILED.

Fix: Update the E2E test to compute the init metadata length, derive ack_offset = ctr_blocks_for_length(metadata_len), and use advance_iv or decrypt_agent_data_ctr to decrypt at the correct offset. Similar pattern is already used in listeners.rs:2552-2556.
close_reason: Fixed as part of AES-CTR IV reuse fix (red-2bm)

title: plugins: queue_raw_agent_task generates mismatched request_id and task_id
description: In plugins.rs::queue_raw_agent_task (line 468/471), request_id and task_id are generated by two separate calls to next_request_id(), so they always have different counter values (e.g. request_id=5, task_id=6). task_id should be derived from the same counter value as request_id — e.g. let id = next_request_id(); then use id for request_id and format!("{:08X}", id) for task_id. As-is, any system that correlates a queued job by its task_id will fail.
close_reason: Fixed: queue_raw_agent_task now calls next_request_id() once and uses the same value for both request_id and task_id

title: parse_api_agent_id uses decimal for all-digit agent IDs, breaking hex-only IDs like 00000010
close_reason: Fixed parse_api_agent_id and parse_agent_id_filter to always parse as hex, matching websocket.rs. Added unit tests.

title: Fix clippy too-many-arguments violation in sockets.rs register_client
description: cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings fails with: error: this function has too many arguments (9/8) at crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:329 in the register_client method. This breaks CI clippy gates. Fix: refactor arguments into a struct (e.g. ClientRegistration) or use #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] with justification.
close_reason: register_client was refactored to accept a PendingClient struct (3 params), clippy now passes clean

title: AES-256-CTR IV reused for every agent packet — keystream reuse
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:154 and crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:462–470

The same key+IV pair stored at DEMON_INIT is reused as-is for every
subsequent encrypt/decrypt call for a given agent.  AES-CTR mode is a
stream cipher: it XORs each plaintext byte with a keystream derived from
(key, counter-starting-at-IV).  Reusing the same IV means the exact same
keystream is generated for every message.

Result: for any two ciphertexts C1 and C2 from the same agent session,
C1 XOR C2 = P1 XOR P2.  An observer who knows one plaintext (e.g. the
deterministic ACK body) can immediately recover the other.

Worst case is with Secure=false listeners where TLS is absent: the
keystream-reuse attack has no outer layer to stop it.

Fix: advance a per-session counter stored alongside the key/IV in
AgentEncryptionInfo, or generate a fresh nonce per message and prepend it
to the ciphertext.  Verify the chosen approach matches the Demon C agent's
counter-advance behavior before shipping.
close_reason: Per-agent AES-CTR block counter tracking implemented. ctr_block_offset in AgentEntry, encrypt_for_agent/decrypt_from_agent on AgentRegistry, updated init/callback/job flows in demon.rs+dispatch.rs+listeners.rs. All 326 tests passing, clippy clean, formatted.

title: ApiRuntime rate-limit windows map grows unboundedly for inactive keys
description:
Introduced by: Cursor Agent <noreply@cursor.com>

crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:100-101 — `ApiRuntime::windows` is a `BTreeMap<String, RateLimitWindow>` that only shrinks when:
- `check_rate_limit` resets a window that has expired AND the key makes another request
- There is no background timer, no prune-on-read loop, and no eviction policy

An API key that triggers rate-limiting once and then disappears will occupy a map entry forever. Under a realistic red-team engagement with rotating keys or brief tool runs, this map accumulates one entry per unique key that was ever rate-limited. The attack surface is bounded by the number of configured API keys (not externally controllable), so OOM is unlikely in practice, but it is the same architectural flaw as the LoginRateLimiter bug (red-cell-c2-2b9) and should be fixed consistently.

Fix: add a periodic prune pass (drop expired windows) or cap total map size, consistent with how the LoginRateLimiter fix was or will be approached.
close_reason: Pruned expired REST API rate-limit windows on each check and added regression coverage.

title: audit: query_audit_log loads entire table into memory before filtering
description:
crates/teamserver/src/audit.rs:187-208 — query_audit_log calls database.audit_log().list().await? which fetches all rows from the database, converts every row to AuditRecord, reverses the entire Vec, then applies in-memory filtering and slice-based pagination.

For a busy teamserver with thousands of agent tasks logged per day, this will:
- Consume unbounded memory proportional to total audit log size
- Increase latency linearly with log size even for small page requests
- Convert and deserialize all rows even when only 1 page of 50 is needed

Fix: push filtering and pagination into SQL. The audit_log repository should expose a filtered query method that accepts WHERE clauses for actor, action, target_kind, target_id, and a JSON-extracted agent_id/command/result_status, plus LIMIT and OFFSET. COUNT(*) with the same WHERE can provide the total without fetching all rows.
close_reason: Pushed filtering and pagination into SQL via AuditLogRepository.query_filtered/count_filtered; removed in-memory filtering from query_audit_log

title: extract_external_ip trusts agent-supplied X-Real-IP header when behind_redirector=false
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com> (original HTTP listener implementation)

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2044-2062 — `extract_external_ip()`

When `config.behind_redirector` is false, the function falls back to the `x-real-ip` header value. `X-Real-IP` is conventionally injected by a reverse proxy (nginx, etc.) but in this code path, the source is the agent itself (a direct TCP connection). Any Demon agent — or an attacker spoofing one — can set `X-Real-IP: 1.2.3.4` in its HTTP request and that value becomes the recorded `external_ip` in the agent registry and audit log.

This is related to but distinct from red-cell-c2-xpe ("always 0.0.0.0"): when an agent does set this header, the stored IP is an attacker-controlled string, not the true TCP peer address. This allows phantom agents to record arbitrary IPs, polluting the operator's situational awareness.

The `X-Real-IP` fallback should be removed from the `behind_redirector=false` path. The fix for red-cell-c2-xpe (using Axum's ConnectInfo to get the actual TCP peer address) would supersede this fallback entirely.
close_reason: Fixed by 88ca055 — extract_external_ip now always uses peer.ip() when behind_redirector=false; X-Real-IP and X-Forwarded-For headers are only read when behind_redirector=true

title: handle_pivot_callback has 9 args — fails clippy too_many_arguments
description:
crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:438 — handle_pivot_callback now takes 9 parameters after the PluginRuntime argument was added (registry, events, database, sockets, downloads, plugins, agent_id, request_id, payload). Clippy's too_many_arguments lint fires (limit is 8) and -D warnings promotes it to an error, breaking 'cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings'.

Fix: introduce a DispatchContext struct (or similar) to bundle the shared state refs (registry, events, database, sockets, downloads, plugins) and pass it as a single argument, reducing the parameter count below the limit.
close_reason: Resolved while implementing the embedded PyO3 runtime: plugin tests were stabilized and pivot dispatch now uses a shared context, so cargo test and cargo clippy both pass again.

title: build_job silently maps invalid task_id to 0 on parse failure
description: In crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs line 600, `u32::from_str_radix(info.task_id.trim(), 16).unwrap_or_default()` silently maps any malformed or non-hex task_id string to request_id=0. If a client sends a job with an invalid task_id, the resulting callback is stored under task 0, making it unroutable and invisible to the operator. This should return an `AgentCommandError::InvalidNumericField` (the same error path used for other numeric fields like `parse_hex_u32` at line 1152) rather than silently defaulting.
close_reason: Duplicate of red-cell-c2-1xg — already fixed in commit ec7ad40. build_job now returns AgentCommandError::InvalidTaskId instead of unwrap_or_default(), with tests for empty, non-hex, and overflowing task_id values.

title: DNS listener silently drops non-C2-domain queries instead of responding with REFUSED
description:
In listeners.rs, DnsListenerState::handle_dns_packet uses the ? operator on parse_dns_c2_query, which returns None when labels don't match the configured C2 domain. This causes the whole function to return None, so no UDP response is sent to the client. The failing test (dns_listener_starts_and_responds_to_unknown_queries_with_refused) demonstrates this: it sends a query for 'something.other.domain.com' to a listener configured for 'c2.example.com' and expects a REFUSED (RCODE=5) response, but times out instead.

Fix: replace 'let c2_query = parse_dns_c2_query(&query.labels, &self.config.domain)?;' with explicit None handling that falls through to 'Some(build_dns_refused_response(query.id))' when the domain doesn't match.

This is a P1 bug because it causes a test failure and means non-C2 DNS traffic going to the listener is silently dropped instead of being refused, which can confuse legitimate DNS resolvers expecting a response.
close_reason: Returned REFUSED for non-matching DNS queries instead of dropping the packet silently

title: request_contexts HashMap in AgentRegistry is never pruned — unbounded memory growth
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:79,286,345 — Every call to `enqueue_job` inserts a `JobContext` entry into the `request_contexts` HashMap keyed by `(agent_id, request_id)`. The entry is never removed after being read by `request_context` (called from `dispatch.rs:794`). Over a long engagement with many tasked agents, this map grows without bound, slowly consuming memory.

Fix: remove the entry from `request_contexts` inside `request_context` after it is read (change from `.get` to `.remove`), or add a bounded eviction policy (e.g., retain only the last N contexts per agent).
close_reason: Bounded request_contexts eviction (MAX 10k entries, oldest-half pruned), plus lifecycle cleanup on mark_dead/remove. All 238 tests pass.

title: Client - UI Components
description: All egui UI panels and widgets: login dialog, main window layout, session table (agent list), session graph (node visualization), command console (interactive per-agent shell), file browser, process list, loot viewer, and multi-operator chat panel.

title: Dead zero-key plaintext passthrough in encrypt/decrypt functions is unreachable and contradicts decode_crypto_material error
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

**What is wrong:**
In `crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs`, three functions have a dead-code block immediately after calling `decode_crypto_material`:

- `encrypt_for_agent` (line 348–350)
- `encrypt_for_agent_without_advancing` (line 374–376)
- `decrypt_from_agent` (line 397–399)

Each contains:
```rust
if key.iter().all(|byte| *byte == 0) {
    return Ok(plaintext.to_vec()); // plaintext passthrough (or ciphertext passthrough for decrypt)
}
```

This code is unreachable because `decode_crypto_material` (called just above via `?`) already returns `Err(TeamserverError::InvalidAgentCrypto)` for all-zero keys, causing early return before this block. The block can never execute.

**Why it matters:**
- Dead code is confusing: a reader unfamiliar with `decode_crypto_material`'s zero-key rejection would think these functions silently skip encryption for zero-key agents (plaintext passthrough instead of error).
- The behaviour is actively wrong if somehow reached (encryption bypass vs. error). This directly contradicts the zero-key security policy established by `decode_crypto_material` and tested in `zero_key_agent_transport_is_rejected`.
- The three dead blocks should simply be removed.

**Correct behaviour:** Remove the `if key.iter().all(|byte| *byte == 0)` dead-code blocks from `encrypt_for_agent`, `encrypt_for_agent_without_advancing`, and `decrypt_from_agent`. The `decode_crypto_material` rejection is sufficient and the check is already verified by the existing unit test.
close_reason: Removed unreachable zero-key passthrough branches from agent transport helpers; zero-key rejection now relies solely on decode_crypto_material and existing tests.

title: DownloadTracker created fresh per route call — download state never persists
description:
In route_callback_by_command (dispatch.rs:603), a new DownloadTracker::default() is created on every call. This means any multi-chunk download reassembly state stored in the tracker would be immediately discarded at the end of each invocation — chunks would never accumulate into a complete file.

The DownloadTracker should be:
1. Passed in as a parameter from the shared instance in CommandDispatcher::with_handlers (line 131), OR
2. Stored on the shared state struct that route_callback_by_command is called from.

Using a per-call fresh tracker defeats the entire purpose of the HashMap<(u32, u32), DownloadState>. Note that route_callback_by_command at line 603 appears to be a legacy dispatch path; the handler-registration approach in with_handlers (line 131) uses the shared downloads instance. If route_callback_by_command is still active, it must share the same DownloadTracker.
close_reason: Implemented the missing filesystem callback handlers and fixed shared download tracker reuse in builtin dispatch

title: encode_pivot_job_payload and write_len_prefixed_bytes silently corrupt packets on oversized input
description:
Three length-prefix writes use `u32::try_from(len).unwrap_or_default()`, which silently emits 0 instead of an error when the slice length exceeds u32::MAX (4 GiB):
- crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:544 (payload.len() in encode_pivot_job_payload)
- crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:552 (inner.len() in encode_pivot_job_payload)
- crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:1170 (write_len_prefixed_bytes)

If this code path is ever reached with an oversized buffer, the agent receives a length field of 0 and misreads the framing of all subsequent fields, silently corrupting the protocol stream. These helpers should return a Result and propagate an error rather than defaulting to 0.
close_reason: Fixed: encode_pivot_job_payload and write_len_prefixed_bytes now return Result<_, TeamserverError::PayloadTooLarge> instead of silently writing 0 on oversized input

title: Reconcile Demon transport crypto mode with Havoc CTR reference
description: Issue red-cell-c2-499 requested AES-256-CBC helpers in crates/common, but the Havoc reference implementation currently uses AES-CTR for Demon traffic on both the teamserver and agent sides (see src/Havoc/teamserver/pkg/common/crypt/aes.go and src/Havoc/payloads/Demon/src/crypt/AesCrypt.c). Before wiring crypto into packet parsing and handshake code, verify the intended on-wire mode for Red Cell and either switch the implementation or document an intentional protocol divergence.
close_reason: Verified Havoc uses AES-CTR, switched shared agent crypto helpers to CTR, and corrected protocol docs

title: Client - Python API
description: Embed Python (via PyO3) in the client for operator scripting. Python script manager UI panel, custom command registration from scripts, event callbacks, and access to agent/listener objects from Python. Preserves compatibility with existing Havoc client-side Python modules.

title: "Erro" truncated event type in kerberos and socket callback handlers
description:
In crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs, 8 error events use "Erro" instead of "Error" as the Type field sent to operator clients. The Havoc operator client relies on this string for color-coding and display formatting — "Error" (red), "Good" (green), "Info" (blue). Using "Erro" means error conditions are silently shown with no color coding.

Affected lines in dispatch.rs: 610, 635, 657, 673, 721, 772, 793, 815

Handlers affected: handle_kerberos_callback (Luid, Klist, Purge, Ptt subcommands) and handle_socket_callback (ReversePortForward start/clear, Read, Write failure branches).

Fix: replace all instances of "Erro" with "Error" in agent_response_event kind arguments in these handlers.
close_reason: Fixed all 9 truncated "Erro" → "Error" event types in kerberos callback (Luid, Klist, Purge, Ptt), socket callback (ReversePortForward start/clear, Read, Write), and filesystem cat handlers

title: audit: update_listener and delete_listener REST endpoints missing audit trail
description:
The audit trail implementation in 001245d covers listener.create, listener.start, and listener.stop for both the REST API and WebSocket paths, but update_listener (PUT /api/v1/listeners/{name}) and delete_listener (DELETE /api/v1/listeners/{name}) are not audited.

Both handlers still use _identity (underscore-prefixed, identity ignored) and contain no record_audit_entry calls. A listener deletion is a security-relevant event that must appear in the audit log.

Affected files:
- crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:929-941 (update_listener) 
- crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:958-965 (delete_listener)

Fix: change _identity to identity in both handlers and add record_audit_entry calls for success and failure paths, consistent with the pattern already used by create_listener / start_listener / stop_listener.
close_reason: Added audit trail entries to update_listener and delete_listener REST handlers with success/failure recording, consistent with existing listener audit pattern

title: HTTP 429 rate-limit response missing Retry-After header
description: In crates/teamserver/src/api.rs, when the rate limit is exceeded the ApiAuthError::RateLimited variant serialises retry_after_seconds into the JSON response body, but the HTTP Retry-After header (RFC 9110 §10.2.3) is never set on the response. HTTP clients, proxies, and rate-limit-aware libraries look for this header and will not back off correctly without it. Fix: in the IntoResponse implementation for ApiAuthError::RateLimited, add the Retry-After header to the response with value retry_after_seconds before returning the JSON body.
close_reason: Added Retry-After header to the HTTP 429 response in the ApiAuthError::RateLimited IntoResponse impl, with test coverage

title: auth: error message has typo "exits" instead of "exist"
description: auth.rs line 89: AuthenticationFailure::UnknownUser returns "User doesn't exits" — should be "User doesn't exist". The typo is surfaced directly to the operator login UI and audit log. Introduced in commit 8664e86 by michel@woodenshoe.org. P3 severity.
close_reason: Corrected the unknown-user authentication error text and updated the auth message test.

title: plugins: silent mutex poison recovery in install_as_active/active() can mask panics
description: In crates/teamserver/src/plugins.rs lines 171-188, both install_as_active() and active() recover a poisoned Mutex by calling poisoned.into_inner(). A poisoned mutex means another thread panicked while holding the lock, leaving the guarded value in an unknown state. Silently recovering it means the plugin runtime could be left in a corrupted state with no indication. The correct approach is to treat a poisoned lock as a fatal error (return Err or panic with a clear message), not silently ignore it. This could cause subtle, hard-to-debug correctness issues.
close_reason: install_as_active() and current() now return PluginError::MutexPoisoned instead of silently recovering via into_inner(). All callers propagate the error.

title: websocket: execute_agent_task has triplicated job-queuing block
description: In websocket.rs::execute_agent_task (lines 633-654), the three branches (plugin command returns false, plugin present but no command matched, no plugin runtime) each contain an identical 5-line block: build_jobs -> queue all -> return count. This should be extracted into a shared path to avoid divergence. If one branch is fixed (e.g. error handling change) the others are silently missed.
close_reason: Deduplicated triplicated job-queuing block into a single shared path with a handled_by_plugin boolean

title: read_smb_frame allocates unbounded heap buffer from untrusted length field
description:
In listeners.rs, read_smb_frame (line 1173) reads a 32-bit length field directly from the SMB pipe stream and immediately allocates 'vec![0_u8; payload_len]' without any upper-bound check. A malicious or malfunctioning client can send a length of up to u32::MAX (~4 GB), triggering a multi-gigabyte heap allocation that will likely panic or OOM-kill the teamserver process.

Fix: add a sanity cap before the allocation. A reasonable limit is the maximum Demon packet size (e.g. 16 MiB or whatever is used for the HTTP path). Return an io::Error(InvalidData) if payload_len exceeds that cap, which will cause handle_smb_connection to log a warning and close the connection cleanly.

Location: crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs, read_smb_frame function, line ~1184-1187.
close_reason: Rejected oversized SMB frame lengths before allocation and added regression coverage.

title: No brute-force protection on WebSocket operator login
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:229-357 — The WebSocket authentication handler (`handle_authentication`) has no rate limiting, failed-attempt counter, or delay. An attacker can connect, attempt a login, disconnect on failure, and immediately reconnect — repeating at network speed to brute-force operator passwords.

The REST API has per-key rate limiting (`ApiRateLimit`) but the WebSocket path does not.

Fix: maintain a per-source-IP (or global) failed-login counter backed by a sliding-window or token-bucket, and add a short (e.g., 2 s) delay before responding to failed login attempts.
close_reason: Added per-IP LoginRateLimiter with sliding 60s window (5 max failures), 2-second delay on failed auth, ConnectInfo IP extraction, and tests

title: Implement WebSocket command handlers (agent tasking)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle operator commands for agent interaction. AgentTask: validate agent exists, check operator RBAC permission, enqueue job in agent's job queue, broadcast AgentTask event to all operators. AgentKill. AgentNote (add note to agent). AgentRemove. All with proper RBAC checks.
close_reason: Implemented operator WebSocket handlers for agent task, note, kill, and remove commands with RBAC checks and registry updates

title: QA runner: cargo test fails without RUSTFLAGS — python3.12-dev not installed
description:
The QA review environment is missing `python3.12-dev` (libpython3.12.so dev symlink). Running `cargo test --workspace` fails with:

  rust-lld: error: unable to find library -lpython3.12

The workaround is to run with:
  RUSTFLAGS="-L /usr/lib/python3.12/config-3.12-x86_64-linux-gnu" cargo test --workspace

Fix: install `python3.12-dev` on the QA/dev machines, OR add a `.cargo/config.toml` at the repo root with:
  [build]
  rustflags = ["-L", "/usr/lib/python3.12/config-3.12-x86_64-linux-gnu"]

All 476 tests pass once the library path is provided. This is a CI/environment configuration issue, not a code bug.
close_reason: Added a repo-level .cargo/config.toml with the Python 3.12 linker path for QA hosts missing python3.12-dev linker defaults.

title: Teamserver - Authentication & RBAC
description: Operator authentication (SHA3-256 password hashing, session management) and role-based access control system. Operators should have differentiated permissions (e.g. admin, operator, analyst/read-only). This is a new feature beyond what Havoc provides.

title: Implement Demon payload builder
description: In crates/teamserver: wrap the Demon agent compilation pipeline in Rust. Given a listener config and Demon options (sleep, jitter, sleep obfuscation technique, injection settings, output format), invoke MinGW and NASM to compile a Demon binary. Stream build output to operator. Deliver compiled EXE/DLL/shellcode bytes back via WebSocket. Validate compiler toolchain availability at startup.
close_reason: Implemented a validated Demon payload builder with WebSocket streaming, artifact delivery, and startup toolchain checks

title: Implement AES-256-CBC crypto for agent communication
description: In crates/common: implement AES-256-CBC encryption/decryption for Demon agent communications. Each agent has a unique 32-byte key and 16-byte IV. Use the aes + cbc crates (RustCrypto). Functions: encrypt_agent_data(key, iv, plaintext) -> Vec<u8>, decrypt_agent_data(key, iv, ciphertext) -> Result<Vec<u8>>. Key generation for new agents. Write unit tests verifying interoperability with known Havoc-produced ciphertext.
close_reason: Implemented AES-256-CBC agent crypto helpers in crates/common with encryption, decryption, key generation, and unit tests.

title: Set up egui client binary and app skeleton
description: In crates/client: main.rs entry point for red-cell-client. eframe/egui app setup with window configuration (title, size, dark theme). App state struct holding: connection status, operator info, list of agents, listeners, loot, chat messages. Placeholder panels for all UI sections. CLI args: --server <url> for teamserver address.
close_reason: Implemented the red-cell-client egui app skeleton, CLI server argument, and placeholder panels.

title: client: note task sent with empty operator username
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

In `render_note_editor` (crates/client/src/main.rs:862), `build_note_task` is called with `""` as the operator name:

    self.session_panel.pending_messages.push(build_note_task(&agent_id, &note, ""));

The AppState is already locked on lines 858-861 to call `update_agent_note`. The operator username is available as `state.operator_info.as_ref().map(|op| op.username.as_str())`. The empty string is silently forwarded in the `MessageHead.user` field, so the teamserver receives a note-update task attributed to an anonymous/blank user — breaking audit attribution for note operations.

Fix: read `operator_info.username` from the same lock used for `update_agent_note` and pass it to `build_note_task`.
close_reason: Passed operator_info.username through note task messages so note updates retain operator attribution.

title: plugins test panics: Tokio runtime shutting down during Python GIL call
description:
crates/teamserver/src/plugins.rs — test initialize_exposes_agent_and_listener_accessors fails with:
  'A Tokio 1.x context was found, but it is being shutdown.'

Root cause: the test uses std::thread::spawn to call Python::with_gil, but the Python API functions (get_agent, get_listener) internally call Handle::block_on() using the Handle captured at PluginRuntime::initialize() time. By the time the OS thread executes the GIL block, the #[tokio::test] runtime may already be shutting down, causing the block_on() to panic.

Fix: replace std::thread::spawn with tokio::task::spawn_blocking (consistent with the production code in PluginRuntime::initialize), or restructure the test so the Tokio runtime is alive for the duration of the Python call.

title: Timing attack: password hash comparison uses non-constant-time eq_ignore_ascii_case
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/auth.rs:135 — The login handler compares SHA3-256 password hashes with `eq_ignore_ascii_case`, which is a variable-time string comparison. An attacker making many login attempts can use response timing to deduce partial information about the stored hash, enabling a timing-based oracle attack.

Fix: replace with `subtle::ConstantTimeEq` or compare the lower-hex digests with a constant-time byte comparison. The `subtle` crate is already used in the dependency tree via ring. At minimum convert both sides to lowercase and use `subtle::ConstantTimeEq` on the byte slices.
close_reason: Replaced variable-time password hash comparison with a constant-time comparison and added coverage for uppercase SHA3 digests.

title: payload_builder: negative kill-date timestamps silently wrap to very large u64 values
description: payload_builder.rs lines 613 and 690: parse_kill_date() returns i64, which is then cast directly to u64 via 'as u64'. In Rust 2024 edition, a negative timestamp (e.g. KillDate = "-1" submitted by an operator) wraps to u64::MAX, embedding a kill date of year ~584 billion in the compiled Demon payload — effectively disabling the kill date silently instead of returning an error. Introduced by michel@woodenshoe.org. P3 correctness: pre-1970 kill dates are not a practical use case for C2 payloads, but the implicit integer wrapping is unexpected. A u64::try_from() with an error would be safer.

title: SocksServerHandle::shutdown ignores graceful shutdown channel, always aborts task
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:499-501 — The SocksServerHandle struct stores a oneshot::Sender<()> in its 'shutdown' field, and the spawned accept loop uses a tokio::select! to listen for this channel. However, the shutdown() method never sends on the channel:

    fn shutdown(&self) {
        self.task.abort();  // always aborts, never sends shutdown_tx
    }

The Drop impl does send on it, but also calls task.abort(). The net result is that graceful shutdown via the channel is never used — tasks are always abruptly aborted, which could leave the TCP listener in an uncleaned state.

Fix: have shutdown() send on the oneshot channel instead of (or before) aborting, so the accept loop can terminate cleanly.
close_reason: SocksServerHandle::shutdown now sends the graceful shutdown signal and a regression test covers the accept-loop exit path

Sync with DB on changes. Write unit tests for concurrency.

title: Implement Demon packet parser and AES handshake
description: In crates/teamserver: parse incoming Demon binary packets. Read 12-byte header (Size, Magic=0xDEADBEEF, AgentID). For new agents (DEMON_INIT): handle key exchange to establish per-agent AES-256-CBC key+IV, store in agent registry. For existing agents: look up key by AgentID, decrypt payload, return parsed command ID + request ID + data bytes. All using types from common crate.

title: Workspace fails to compile: api.rs references items only in git stash
description:
The working tree has `crates/teamserver/src/api.rs` (agent REST endpoints) that references:

- `crate::websocket::execute_agent_task` — function not yet pub(crate) in committed code

Running `cargo check --workspace` produces 21 compiler errors. The stash contains the prerequisite changes but has not been applied.

title: agent_new_event duplicated between listeners.rs and dispatch.rs — HTTP/SMB/DNS agents lose pivot chain info
description:
Introduced by: Codex

Two separate private functions named agent_new_event exist:
- crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:907 — 3-arg version (listener_name, magic_value, agent)
  Uses AgentPivotsInfo::default() — always emits empty pivot chain.
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:2450 — 4-arg version with real PivotInfo.

process_demon_transport (listeners.rs) calls the 3-arg version, so any agent that registers via the HTTP, SMB, or DNS listener will broadcast an AgentNew event with no pivot information, even if it is a pivot agent routed through the SMB listener.

This means the operator console will always show these agents as having no parent/children, regardless of actual topology.

Fix: move agent_new_event (and the helper operator_agent_info) into common/domain.rs or a shared listeners/dispatch helper, and have both callers use the shared function with real PivotInfo. The 3-arg variant in listeners.rs should be removed.
close_reason: Centralized agent session event building and preserved pivot metadata for listener and snapshot AgentNew events.

title: Initialize Cargo workspace
description: Create the Cargo workspace root Cargo.toml at the repo root. Set up the workspace members: crates/teamserver, crates/client, crates/common. Create placeholder lib.rs/main.rs for each crate. Update .gitignore for Rust build artifacts (target/). Set up rustfmt.toml and clippy.toml with project-wide lint settings. Rust edition 2024.
close_reason: Initialized the Cargo workspace with placeholder crates and shared lint configuration.

title: Duplicate SHA3-256 password hash implementation in auth.rs vs common/crypto.rs
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com> (feat: implement operator authentication)

crates/teamserver/src/auth.rs:277-286 — `auth::hash_password()`
crates/common/src/crypto.rs:134-145 — `crypto::hash_password_sha3()`

Both functions compute SHA3-256 of a password and return a lowercase hex string. Both produce identical output (verified by the same test vector: `hash('password1234') == '2f7d3e77...')`). The `auth` module uses its own local implementation instead of the common crate's public API.

This violates the DRY principle and creates a maintenance hazard: if one implementation is changed (e.g., to add a salt, PBKDF2 rounds, or encoding change), the other silently stays diverged. Since both functions produce identical output today, no bug exists yet, but this is an architecture drift from the principle that `crates/common` owns shared crypto primitives.

Fix: delete `auth::hash_password` and replace all call sites with `red_cell_common::crypto::hash_password_sha3`.
close_reason: Removed the duplicate teamserver SHA3-256 helper and switched auth call sites to red_cell_common::crypto::hash_password_sha3.

title: Implement agent checkin handler (DEMON_INIT)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle DEMON_INIT (command 99) - the initial agent registration. Parse agent metadata from checkin payload: hostname, username, domain, IPs, PID, PPID, process name, arch, OS version, elevation status, sleep/jitter config. Create AgentInfo, store in registry and DB, broadcast AgentNew event to all operators. Return success response to agent.
close_reason: Handled DEMON_INIT callbacks by persisting agents, returning init acks, and broadcasting AgentNew events.

title: transport.rs: TLS certificate verification completely bypassed
description:
Introduced by: Codex

In `crates/client/src/transport.rs`, the `build_insecure_tls_connector` function uses a `DangerousCertificateVerifier` that accepts any TLS certificate without any validation (line ~817-828). The `verify_server_cert` implementation always returns `Ok(ServerCertVerified::assertion())` unconditionally.

There is no alternative path for operators to provide a CA certificate or pin the server certificate. This leaves operator credentials (username + SHA3-256 hashed password) and all C2 traffic vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks on untrusted networks.

Expected fix: support at minimum one of:
1. Certificate pinning (operator stores a fingerprint of the teamserver cert on first connection)
2. Custom CA certificate path in the client config
3. `--accept-invalid-cert` flag that must be explicitly opted into rather than being the only option
close_reason: Replaced unconditional TLS bypass with TlsVerification enum: CertificateAuthority (default), CustomCa, Fingerprint pinning, and explicit DangerousSkipVerify. Added CLI flags --accept-invalid-certs, --ca-cert, --cert-fingerprint with config file fallbacks. 10 new tests covering all modes.

title: audit: OperatorMessage::ListenerRemove WebSocket path missing audit trail
description:
The WebSocket dispatch_operator_command function gained audit logging for ListenerNew and ListenerMark in 001245d, but OperatorMessage::ListenerRemove (crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:597-607) has no audit calls on either the success or failure branch.

title: Unauthenticated DEMON_INIT reconnect probe desyncs agent AES-CTR counter
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com> (feat: add demon packet parser)

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:976-985 / crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:136-137

The Demon protocol's reconnect probe is a DEMON_INIT packet with an empty payload (20 bytes total: 12-byte header + 4-byte command_id + 4-byte request_id). The parser returns `ParsedDemonPacket::Reconnect` for this shape.

`process_demon_transport` then calls `build_init_ack(registry, header.agent_id)` if the agent_id is known. `build_init_ack` calls `registry.encrypt_for_agent()`, which **advances the teamserver's CTR block offset by 1** (4 bytes → 1 block).

**Attack**: Any attacker who can reach the HTTP listener port and knows a registered agent's 32-bit agent_id (visible in plaintext in every Demon packet header when TLS is not used) can send crafted 20-byte reconnect probes to repeatedly advance the server's CTR counter for the target agent, while the real agent's counter stays unchanged. After N forged probes, the server encrypts responses at offset N while the agent expects offset 0. All subsequent comms are silently broken — the agent receives garbage and cannot communicate.

No knowledge of the agent's AES key is required. The agent_id is the only secret.

**Fix**: Reconnect probe handling should not advance the CTR counter. The ACK for a reconnect should either use offset 0 (if the agent is confirming it still holds the correct key) or require the probe to carry a keyed proof-of-knowledge before advancing state. Alternatively, require a valid HMAC/proof before treating a packet as an authenticated reconnect.
close_reason: Reconnect ACKs now use a non-mutating CTR preview path so unauthenticated probes cannot desync agent transport state.

--- HANDOFF NOTES (2026-03-10, interrupted session) ---

Commit 7fa28af (feat(teamserver): partial DNS C2 listener implementation) contains a partial but functional foundation. Do NOT restart from scratch — build on this.

Work through the open sub-issues above, then run the full test suite before closing this issue.

title: encode_token_payload PrivsGetOrList discards SubCommand from extras — privs-list unreachable via extras map
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-HTB-cursor (Cursor Agent)

In `crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs` around line 1374, the `PrivsGetOrList` arm of `encode_token_payload` reads SubCommand from `info.extra` but discards the value:

```rust
flat_info_string_from_extra(&info.extra, &["SubCommand"]).as_deref().map(|_| "")
```

The `.map(|_| "")` replaces any non-empty value with an empty string `""`. This means when `info.sub_command` is None and the client sends SubCommand in extras (e.g., `"privs-list"` or `"4"`), the variable `sub` ends up as `""`, which never matches `"privs-list"` or `"4"`, so the function always takes the get-privileges path (writes 0 + requires privilege name argument) even when the client intended list-privileges (should write 1).

The correct code should bind the returned String before calling `.as_deref()` so the lifetime is preserved, then use the actual value in the comparison:
```rust
let sub_from_extra = flat_info_string_from_extra(&info.extra, &["SubCommand"]);
let sub = info.sub_command.as_deref().or_else(|| sub_from_extra.as_deref()).unwrap_or("");
```

There is no unit test for this code path in websocket tests, so the bug went undetected.
close_reason: Preserved SubCommand from extras in token payload encoding and added websocket regression tests for privs-list.

title: DNS pending responses not expired if agent dies mid-download
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1337 — DnsListenerState.responses (HashMap<u32, DnsPendingResponse>) is only cleaned up in handle_download when the seq index goes past the end of the chunk list (line 1470). There is no timestamp on DnsPendingResponse, so if an agent requests seq=0 then dies, its response entry remains in the map indefinitely.

The cleanup_expired_uploads task only prunes the 'uploads' map; it ignores 'responses'.

This is distinct from the previously closed red-cell-c2-2jw (which fixed the case where responses were never removed after full retrieval). The residual case is partial retrieval: agent starts download at seq=0 but the connection drops.

Fix: add a received_at timestamp to DnsPendingResponse and include response map cleanup in cleanup_expired_uploads, using the same DNS_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS threshold or a separate configurable TTL.
close_reason: Added response timestamps and cleanup for stale DNS pending downloads.

title: DNS listeners from profile are silently ignored at startup
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

crates/teamserver/src/main.rs:260-266 — `profile_listener_names` collects HTTP, SMB, and External listener names but omits DNS listeners. Any DNS listener defined in the `.yaotl` profile is therefore never started by `start_profile_listeners`.

DNS support was added after this function was written and the DNS case was not back-filled.
